extracted_codes.context: International Classification of Diseases, Ninth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-9-CM) diagnoses codes (Supplemental Appendix 1, available online at http://www.mcpiqojournal.org)

clinical_criteria: Laboratory test values (specific thresholds not provided in main document)
Positive annotation of DM in patients' clinical notes using keyword-based search
HbA1c values (referenced as 'last HbA1c unknown' and 'HbA1c, 131, January 17, 2014' in example notes)
algorithm_summary: The phenotyping algorithm uses first-order logic rules (if-then-else rules) to identify patients with type 1 or type 2 diabetes mellitus preoperatively. The algorithm combines four main data sources: (1) ICD-9-CM diagnostic codes, (2) outpatient prescription information, (3) laboratory values, and (4) positive annotation of diabetes mellitus in patients' clinical notes using natural language processing with keyword-based search. The 'if' clause of each rule uses logical AND/OR predicates to provide evidence toward or against the presence of diabetes. The algorithm achieved 99.70% sensitivity and 99.97% specificity when validated against expert review in a cohort of 4,208 surgical patients, identifying 684 patients (16.25%) as having diabetes mellitus.
